### Post by RockTonic3 on 2007-09-26
has anybody gotten suspend or hibernate to work on their XPS M170?

For me, everything seems to go well until i try to resume, and get nothing but a mostly black screen with a little jumbled box wherever the cursor is.  

Here are the contents of my xorg.conf:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "Files"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"

	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"nVidia Corporation GE Force Go 7800 GTX"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"AddARGBVisuals"	"True"
	Option		"AddARGBGLXVisuals"	"True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	Option		"DPMS"
	Horizsync	28-96
	Vertrefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"

	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	1
		Modes		"1920x1200"

		Modes		"1920x1200"

	EndSubSection

		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"

	Inputdevice	"stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

And here is my acpi-support...

```

# Comment the next line to disable ACPI suspend to RAM
ACPI_SLEEP=true

# Comment the next line to disable suspend to disk
ACPI_HIBERNATE=true

# Change the following to "standby" to use ACPI S1 sleep, rather than S3.
# This will save less power, but may work on more machines
ACPI_SLEEP_MODE=mem

# Add modules to this list to have them removed before suspend and reloaded
# on resume. An example would be MODULES="em8300 yenta_socket"
#
# Note that network cards and USB controllers will automatically be unloaded 
# unless they're listed in MODULES_WHITELIST
MODULES=""

# Add modules to this list to leave them in the kernel over suspend/resume
MODULES_WHITELIST=""

# Should we save and restore state using the VESA BIOS Extensions?
SAVE_VBE_STATE=true

# The file that we use to save the vbestate
VBESTATE=/var/lib/acpi-support/vbestate

# Should we attempt to warm-boot the video hardware on resume?
POST_VIDEO=true

# Save and restore video state?
# SAVE_VIDEO_PCI_STATE=true

# Should we switch the screen off with DPMS on suspend?
USE_DPMS=true

# Use Radeontool to switch the screen off? Seems to be needed on some machines
# RADEON_LIGHT=true

# Uncomment the next line to switch away from X and back again after resume.
# This is needed for some hardware, but should be unnecessary on most.
# DOUBLE_CONSOLE_SWITCH=true

# Set the following to "platform" if you want to use ACPI to shut down
# your machine on hibernation
HIBERNATE_MODE=shutdown

# Comment this out to disable screen locking on resume
LOCK_SCREEN=true

# Uncomment this line to have DMA disabled before suspend and reenabled
# afterwards
# DISABLE_DMA=true

# Uncomment this line to attempt to reset the drive on resume. This seems
# to be needed for some Sonys
# RESET_DRIVE=true

# Add services to this list to stop them before suspend and restart them in 
# the resume process.
STOP_SERVICES="mysql "

# Restart Infra Red services on resume - off by default as it crashes some
# machines
RESTART_IRDA=false

# Switch to laptop-mode on battery power - off by default as it causes odd
# hangs on some machines
ENABLE_LAPTOP_MODE=false
```

I've tried to play around with this one a little...  but really don't know what i'm doing!  i started out thinking i'd try different combinations till it worked but gave up on that crazy idea...  hopefully someone out there has more patience than me!  

Thanks for any help

### Post by TalioGladius on 2007-09-26
If you mean XPS 1710 I'm in the same boat.  Goes into suspend without a problem, but when I open the screen and try to wake it all I get is a black screen.  Only way to restore it is to power it off and reboot.....

### Post by RockTonic3 on 2007-09-26
no i mean m170..  it's the older one.  I understand it's a common issue with ubuntu so far...  It could probably be fixed if i knew what to modify in acpi-support, but i'm clueless.  

what gpu do you have?  i have a 7800 gtx.  the settings to make it work would probably be similar for both of us if we have similar hardware, which is probably the case since they're both xps.  i'm not sure how different the 1710 is (i think it's the generation immediately after mine).  

has anybody else with an xps in general (and nvidia) gotten suspend to work?

### Post by Mountaineer on 2007-09-27
I've got an Inspiron 1720 which is fairly similarly specced to the XPS range (I've got an 8600M GT video card for example).

If I choose suspend from the shutdown button menu, it will wake up when I later lift the lid.
But if I just close the lid and later open it up, I get the same black screen with a fuzzy box where the cursor is that you guys mention.

This is with both Feisty Fawn and now Gutsy.

---

### Post by RockTonic3 on 2007-09-27
interesting...  would you mind posting the contents of your acpi-support file?

just type in terminal:

```
sudo gedit /etc/default/acpi-support
```

maybe your settings are different than mine

---

### Post by RockTonic3 on 2007-09-27
update:

In acpi-support, enable the restart x thing, that worked for me!  

```
sudo gedit /etc/default/acpi-support
```

then in the file, find this section and make sure the bottom line is uncommented (remove the hash mark)

```
# Uncomment the next line to switch away from X and back again after resume.
# This is needed for some hardware, but should be unnecessary on most.
  DOUBLE_CONSOLE_SWITCH=true
```

---

### Post by Mountaineer on 2007-10-02
Sorry for the delay in response, I expected the forums to notify me if someone responded to the thread after I posted in it...

anyway, my /etc/default/acpi-support contains:

```

# Comment the next line to disable ACPI suspend to RAM
ACPI_SLEEP=true

# Comment the next line to disable suspend to disk
ACPI_HIBERNATE=true

# Change the following to "standby" to use ACPI S1 sleep, rather than S3.
# This will save less power, but may work on more machines
ACPI_SLEEP_MODE=mem

# Add modules to this list to have them removed before suspend and reloaded
# on resume. An example would be MODULES="em8300 yenta_socket"
#
# Note that network cards and USB controllers will automatically be unloaded 
# unless they're listed in MODULES_WHITELIST
MODULES=""

# Add modules to this list to leave them in the kernel over suspend/resume
MODULES_WHITELIST=""

# Should we save and restore state using the VESA BIOS Extensions?
SAVE_VBE_STATE=true

# The file that we use to save the vbestate
VBESTATE=/var/lib/acpi-support/vbestate

# Should we attempt to warm-boot the video hardware on resume?
POST_VIDEO=true

# Save and restore video state?
# SAVE_VIDEO_PCI_STATE=true

# Should we switch the screen off with DPMS on suspend?
USE_DPMS=true

# Use Radeontool to switch the screen off? Seems to be needed on some machines
# RADEON_LIGHT=true

# Uncomment the next line to switch away from X and back again after resume.
# This is needed for some hardware, but should be unnecessary on most.
# DOUBLE_CONSOLE_SWITCH=true

# Set the following to "platform" if you want to use ACPI to shut down
# your machine on hibernation
HIBERNATE_MODE=shutdown

# Comment this out to disable screen locking on resume
LOCK_SCREEN=true

# Uncomment this line to have DMA disabled before suspend and reenabled
# afterwards
# DISABLE_DMA=true

# Uncomment this line to attempt to reset the drive on resume. This seems
# to be needed for some Sonys
# RESET_DRIVE=true

# Add services to this list to stop them before suspend and restart them in 
# the resume process.
STOP_SERVICES=""

# Restart Infra Red services on resume - off by default as it crashes some
# machines
RESTART_IRDA=false

# Switch to laptop-mode on battery power - off by default as it causes odd
# hangs on some machines
ENABLE_LAPTOP_MODE=false

# Spindown time on battery
SPINDOWN_TIME=12
```

After a little more testing, it seems that waking from suspension is hit and miss.  It works more than it doesn't but it still fails every now and then.
